commands.sourceSection: DOC-007 §11.4 (command payload, RATIFIED). EVENT BINDING CORRECTED 2026-07-17: this entry read emitsEvent=PwuStateChanged, contradicting this same file's own transitions table (MarkPwuReady -> PwuMarkedReady). The PwuStateChanged binding was inferred from §11.4/§11.5 ADJACENCY and is refuted: (a) the Reference Undertaking §26 Expected Event Trace — the corpus's own 72-step worked example — emits PwuMarkedReady at steps 20 and 33 and NEVER emits PwuStateChanged anywhere; (b) §11.5 declares previousState/newState as REQUIRED payload fields, which are meaningless for a mark-ready event (they would be the constants SHAPING/READY) and necessary only for a GENERIC event; (c) §11.3 'PWU proposed event' mirrors §11.2 'Propose PWU command' by name — §11.5 'PWU state changed event' does not mirror §11.4 'Mark PWU ready command'; (d) DOC-007 §33 requires BOTH PwuMarkedReady and PwuStateChanged as first-slice events, so they are not alternatives; (e) §11 schematizes only 2 of the ~11 PWU commands — it is a first-slice sampler (§16 item 6), not an exhaustive command->event pairing, so adjacency proves nothing. PwuStateChanged belongs to the generic ChangePwuState, which already carries reasonCode. See DOC-002 §8.1 (primary transitions keyed by COMMAND) vs §8.2 (exception transitions keyed by TRIGGER).

PAYLOAD FIELDS: DOC-009 §18.1 (create table artifacts) — RATIFIED and field-complete; transcribed column-for-column, not authored. THE COMMAND ITSELF: UNRATIFIED-AUTHORED under sponsor grant 2026-07-16. DOC-002 §34.3's execution command list and DOC-007 §32's first-slice command list BOTH omit any artifact command, and DOC-002 §26.4 / DOC-007 §33 omit any artifact event — yet CompleteExecutionStepPayload.outputArtifactIds (DOC-007 §16.1) and ExecutionStepSucceededPayload.outputArtifactIds (§16.2) both point at Artifacts, and DOC-009 §18.1 persists them as Professional Work Objects. Nothing in the ratified corpus could bring an Artifact into existence: a dangling reference across three documents. This command closes it (§16 item 6, first-slice). NO state machine is declared because the corpus defines none for the Artifact — DOC-002 has no Artifact interface, no invariant section, and no state machine; `status` is therefore recorded, not driven. Do not borrow Evidence.status.

payloadFields.note: id-ref. Prefix 'art' is AUTHORED — DOC-007 §5.2's 21-entry prefix registry has no artifact entry, while DOC-004 writes 'artifact_01J...' in a Validator-result example. Two unratified spellings; 'art' matches this repo's existing ids.ts registry and test fixtures.
DOC-009 §18.1 'artifact_type text not null'. Open value domain — not an enum.

DOC-009 §18.1 'content_hash text not null'. With storageProvider+storageKey this forms uq_artifact_content, and it is what makes §18.3 immutability ('corrections create a new artifact + new semantic version + supersession link') checkable rather than asserted.
DOC-009 §18.1 'byte_size bigint' — nullable in the ratified table.

id-ref, nullable per DOC-009 §18.1. Guide §8.4's 'producing Attempt/invocation'. CompleteExecutionStepPayload already carries executionAttemptId on the wire, so this is bindable today even though no Execution Attempt OBJECT exists yet (§16 item 23; DOC-009 §10.4 defines that table in full too).
DOC-009 §18.1 'security_classification text not null'; §18.4 requires access to enforce it. Open value domain.
DOC-009 §18.1 'retention_class text not null'; §18.4. Open value domain.
DOC-009 §18.1 'status text not null'. Open value domain; no ratified Artifact state machine.

conflicts.topic: MarkPwuReady -> PwuMarkedReady vs PwuStateChanged (RESOLVED 2026-07-17 — this was never a conflict)
conflicts.resolution: WITHDRAWN AND REVERSED. The prior ruling here read: 'DOC-007 emits generic PwuStateChanged (§11.5...); DOC-002 §26.2 emits semantic PwuMarkedReady with the same SHAPING->READY transition. Recommend emitting the semantic PwuMarkedReady but structurally realizing it with the PwuStateChanged payload... Do not ship both.' Three of its claims are false. (1) 'DOC-002 §26.2 emits PwuMarkedReady with the same transition' — §26.2 is a BARE LIST OF EVENT NAMES. It emits nothing and binds no transition; the transition was supplied by this ruling and attributed to the doc. (2) 'Do not ship both' — DOC-007 §33 Required First-Slice Events lists BOTH PwuMarkedReady and PwuStateChanged, adjacently, as required. The corpus requires exactly what this ruling forbade. (3) The framing 'name drift' presumed one event wearing two names; they are two events. DOC-002 §8 gives the real structure: §8.1 PRIMARY transitions are keyed by a Command column (each has a named event; the reason IS the command), §8.2 EXCEPTION transitions are keyed by a Trigger column with NO command (hence a generic event that must CARRY the reason — that is what reasonCode is for, and the 11 §8.2 Triggers are its natural referent, though the corpus never links them explicitly). The decisive evidence is the Reference Undertaking §26 Expected Event Trace, the corpus's own 72-step worked example: it emits PwuMarkedReady at steps 20 and 33, and PwuStateChanged appears in NO worked trace anywhere in the corpus. CONSEQUENCE: this authored ruling, not the corpus, created the 'markPwuReady cannot fill reasonCode' blocker that parked the event gate. Error shape: silence read as conflict, then a winner picked (cf. the DOC-003/DOC-004 'two catalogs' error, same family).

conflicts.topic: CompleteAssuranceAssessment: one event vs five outcome events
conflicts.resolution: DOC-007 emits a single AssuranceAssessmentCompleted carrying disposition in payload; DOC-002 emits five distinct events (AssuranceAssessmentSatisfied/ConditionallySatisfied/Rejected/Inconclusive/Escalated). Pick one modeling: recommend the 5 named outcome events (they read cleaner into per-state PWU drives) OR keep 1 event with a disposition discriminator — but not both. Command carries ValidatorResult (DOC-007) whose dispositionRecommendation selects the branch.

DOC-007 emits DecisionEffective (PROPOSED->EFFECTIVE); DOC-002 approveDecision emits DecisionApproved (PROPOSED->EFFECTIVE) AND separately defines a time-gated DecisionEffective event. Recommend: DecisionApproved for the authority act, DecisionEffective only when effectiveAt is time-gated apart from approval; if not time-gating, collapse to one.
